Overview Quick start Schema architecture Interactive samples Root schema agent-collection agents example extends extensions link presentation purpose rich-text status system-info component foundation guide pattern theme token accessibility anatomy api content design-specifications document-blocks events guideline import interaction motion principle scale section state steps variant aliases category description extends last-updated links metadata preview since status summary tags thumbnail
Root Properties The DSDS spec version this document conforms to. URI reference to the DSDS JSON Schema for validation. Declares that this DSDS document inherits from another DSDS document — typically a core design system. The base document provides the foundational entities. This document layers on top, adding or extending entities. Enables systems-of-systems architectures where product- or brand-specific systems inherit from a shared core. System-level structured documentation for the design system as a whole — its purpose (when to adopt the system), cross-cutting guidelines, and narrative overviews. Each entry is a general document block: a purpose, guideline, section, accessibility, or content block. This is the same documentBlocks mechanism entities use, scoped to the system rather than a single artifact; scope is signalled by position (root vs. inside an entity). One or more documentation groups. Each group is a named collection that organizes entities into separate typed arrays — components, tokenGroups, themes, foundations, patterns, and guides. Multiple groups let a single DSDS file organize entities into logical sections (e.g., one group for foundations, another for components). All entity arrays are optional; a group only needs a name. Items may be inlined or referenced via a $ref object pointing to another DSDS file.
Min items: 1 A single entity document — one component, token, token group, theme, foundation, pattern, or guide. Use this instead of documentation when each entity lives in its own file. The kind field on the entity identifies its type.

2 definitions in this file:

  • documentationGroup
  • fileRef
 Human-readable name of the collection (e.g., 'Acme Design System', 'Color Tokens', 'Button Documentation'). This is already a display label — it does NOT have an identifier counterpart and need not match a slug pattern. Component entities in this documentation group. Each item describes a UI component — its anatomy, variants, states, accessibility guidelines, and usage rules. Items may be inlined or referenced via a $ref object pointing to another DSDS file. Token group entities in this documentation group. Each item describes a collection of related design tokens — colors, spacing, typography scales, etc. Items may be inlined or referenced via a $ref object pointing to another DSDS file. Theme entities in this documentation group. Each item describes a theme — a named set of token overrides that customize the visual appearance of the system (e.g., light/dark mode, brand variations). Items may be inlined or referenced via a $ref object pointing to another DSDS file. Foundation entities in this documentation group. Each item documents a broad, cross-cutting design domain — color, typography, spacing, elevation, motion, shape, accessibility, content, and the like — with its principles, scales, and guidelines. Items may be inlined or referenced via a $ref object pointing to another DSDS file. Pattern entities in this documentation group. Each item describes a UX pattern — a reusable solution to a common design problem, composed of one or more components (e.g., form validation, empty states, navigation flows). Items may be inlined or referenced via a $ref object pointing to another DSDS file. Guide entities in this documentation group. Each item is a long-form, reading-oriented document — a getting-started walkthrough, a contribution guide, a tutorial, a conceptual overview, or a migration guide. Items may be inlined or referenced via a $ref object pointing to another DSDS file. References: richText, component, fileRef, tokenGroup, theme, foundation, pattern, guide, extensions A relative or absolute URI pointing to a DSDS file, optionally with a JSON Pointer fragment identifying the target value within that file.

Design System Documentation Spec (DSDS) 0.6 — Draft Specification

GitHub
